cards.name: The Broken Column
cards.meaning: The crack runs through you and always has. Stop trying to hold yourself together with silence — the wound is open and it is yours. Name every fracture and you will find that broken things still stand.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have mistaken your ruin for your cathedral. The cracks are real but you have stopped repairing them, choosing instead to display them like relics. Suffering is not a personality.
cards.keywords: cracked ionic column, nails, tears, corset, barren landscape

cards.name: Henry Ford Hospital
cards.meaning: What left your body was real. Grieve it — the blood, the hope, the version of the future that bled out on institutional sheets. You are allowed to mourn what never fully arrived.
cards.reversedMeaning: You are drowning in a grief that has become a closed room. The loss was real but you have locked the door and swallowed the key. Mourning without motion becomes its own tomb.
cards.keywords: hospital bed, red ribbons, floating symbols, snail, orchid

cards.name: The Corset
cards.meaning: Something is holding you upright that you did not choose. Feel it — the brace, the obligation, the structure someone else built around your spine. Now ask yourself: is it saving you or suffocating you?
cards.reversedMeaning: You have ripped away every support and called it freedom. But the spine is not yet healed and the floor is cold. Liberation without readiness is just a different kind of falling.
cards.keywords: plaster corset, painted flowers, bound torso, steel brace, red sickle

cards.name: The Surgical Table
cards.meaning: You are on the table now. There is no dignity in this, only necessity. Let them cut — let the world see the interior you have been protecting. The operation is terrifying but the infection was worse.
cards.reversedMeaning: You keep returning to the table when no surgery is needed. The scalpel has become a comfort, the anesthesia a habit. Not every wound requires reopening — some need only time.
cards.keywords: surgical instruments, ether mask, white sheet, blood, overhead lamp

cards.name: The Spine of Thorns
cards.meaning: The pain has a shape and the shape is yours. Every thorn is a day you survived by refusing to lie down. Your spine is not made of bone alone — it is made of every single time you got back up.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have made yourself untouchable. The thorns that once protected you now pierce anyone who reaches for you. Armor built from pain still draws blood — mostly from those who love you.
cards.keywords: thorn-wrapped spine, roses, blood drops, vertebrae, green vines

cards.name: Without Hope
cards.meaning: You cannot eat what they are feeding you. The world pushes its medicine toward your mouth and it tastes like ash. Say it plainly: this is not nourishment, this is not what I need. Your refusal is a kind of clarity.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have refused every offering until there is nothing left on the table. Rejecting what is unworthy has become rejecting what is necessary. Starving yourself of hope is not the same as being honest.
cards.keywords: funnel, skull, fish, bed, barren landscape, tears

cards.name: Tree of Hope
cards.meaning: Hold your own corset in your hands. You are both the wounded one on the table and the one who sits upright in red velvet, saying: I will survive this. The hope is not naive — it has seen the X-rays.
cards.reversedMeaning: Hope wielded as denial. You hold the banner that reads 'Tree of Hope, Keep Firm' but you have not looked at the wound in months. Optimism that refuses to acknowledge the damage is just a decorated blindfold.
cards.keywords: red velvet dress, banner, crescent moon, two Fridas, surgical scars

cards.name: The Two Fridas
cards.meaning: You are not one person. The self who is loved and the self who is abandoned sit side by side, sharing a single circulatory system. Hold both their hands. Neither one is the lie.
cards.reversedMeaning: The two selves have turned against each other. One bleeds while the other watches with surgical scissors. You are at war with yourself and calling it self-knowledge. The artery between them is nearly severed.
cards.keywords: two seated figures, exposed hearts, artery, scissors, stormy sky

cards.name: Diego and I
cards.meaning: The face you love has taken root in your forehead. They live inside your thoughts whether you invited them or not. This is not weakness — this is what it means to love someone so completely that your skin cannot contain it.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have disappeared inside another person's face. The third eye that should be yours now shows only their image. Devotion has eaten your identity and you are calling the hunger love.
cards.keywords: third eye, face within face, loose hair, tears, tangled roots

cards.name: The Love Embrace
cards.meaning: You are held by something ancient and enormous. The earth, a body, a love so large it has its own geography. Let yourself be small inside it. Let yourself be held without needing to hold anything back.
cards.reversedMeaning: The embrace has become a grip. What once cradled you now controls you, and you have mistaken the pressure for warmth. Being held too tightly is its own kind of suffocation.
cards.keywords: giant arms, earth mother, cradled figure, roots, third eye

cards.name: The Bleeding Heart
cards.meaning: The heart is outside the body now. Everyone can see it. Good — let them see the size of what you have felt. Your capacity for love is grotesque and magnificent and it refuses to be hidden behind bone.
cards.reversedMeaning: You are hemorrhaging and calling it generosity. The heart outside the body was brave once, but now you bleed on everyone who comes near. Vulnerability without boundaries is just a wound with an audience.
cards.keywords: anatomical heart, blood drops, thorns, open chest, red flowers

cards.name: The Ribbon
cards.meaning: The thread that connects you to them is not a leash — it is a vein. It can be cut, but you need to know it will bleed. Choose whether the connection is worth the blood it costs you.
cards.reversedMeaning: The ribbon has become a tourniquet. You have pulled it so tight that nothing flows in either direction. What was once a living connection is now a knot you are afraid to untie because you have forgotten what your circulation felt like without it.
cards.keywords: red ribbon, tangled hair, scissors, two figures, braided thread

cards.name: The Empty Bed
cards.meaning: The bed holds the shape of who was here. Do not rush to smooth the sheets. Sit with the imprint, the warmth that is fading, the specific weight of absence. This emptiness is the exact size of what mattered.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have made a shrine of the empty bed. No one is allowed to lie there, including you. The space you keep sacred has become a room you cannot enter, and the preservation of absence has replaced the possibility of presence.
cards.keywords: iron bed frame, rumpled sheets, empty pillow, Casa Azul, moonlight

cards.name: A Few Small Nips
cards.meaning: Someone will call your devastation small. A few small nips, they will say. But you are covered in wounds and the room is covered in blood. Do not let anyone measure your pain with their ruler. You know what was done to you.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have become the one minimizing. Whether your own pain or someone else's, you wield the phrase 'a few small nips' like a blade. The cruelty of dismissal is that it wounds twice — once in the act, once in the naming.
cards.keywords: knife, dove, bloodstains, bandaged figure, wooden bed

cards.name: Roots
cards.meaning: The roots do not ask permission. They break through stone because growing is not optional. You are planted here — in this body, this soil, this impossible situation — and still the green pushes out of you like a confession.
cards.reversedMeaning: Growth without direction. The roots crack the foundation of something you still needed. Not all breaking-through is progress — sometimes the green thing pushing out of you is destroying the ground you stand on.
cards.keywords: roots from body, cracked earth, red veins, green leaves, reclining figure

cards.name: The Monkey
cards.meaning: The wild thing on your shoulder knows your secrets. It has seen you undressed, unmasked, unraveled. Keep it close — the creature that loves you without language loves you without conditions. Not all companionship requires conversation.
cards.reversedMeaning: The wild thing has turned possessive. What was once companionship has become dependency, and you are no longer sure who is sitting on whose shoulder. The creature comforts you but it also keeps everyone else away.
cards.keywords: spider monkey, yellow ribbon, jungle leaves, dark eyes, shoulder

cards.name: The Wounded Deer
cards.meaning: You are running through the forest with arrows in your body. You have always been running. But look — the antlers are growing, the legs still carry you, and the forest has not swallowed you yet. The hunted thing survives by becoming something with horns.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have identified so completely with the hunted thing that you have forgotten you grew antlers. The arrows are real but you are running toward the hunters now, as if the wound is a destination. Stop. The forest is not the enemy.
cards.keywords: deer body, arrows, antlers, forest, Frida's face, lightning

cards.name: The Parrot
cards.meaning: Speak in colors. Say the unsayable thing with feathers and shrieking and iridescent defiance. The parrot does not whisper — it fills the room with itself. You have been too quiet for too long.
cards.reversedMeaning: Noise mistaken for truth. You are repeating words that are not yours, filling silence with borrowed language. The parrot's mimicry is dazzling but it is not the same as having something to say.
cards.keywords: green parrot, bright feathers, perch, tropical leaves, open beak

cards.name: The Watermelons
cards.meaning: Split yourself open and show the red interior. The sweetness is there, and so are the seeds of what comes next. This is the fruit of a life fully lived — messy, dripping, staining everything it touches. Bite down.
cards.reversedMeaning: Rotting sweetness. The fruit was cut open and left too long in the sun. What was once ripe and honest has fermented into something performative. You are offering overripe pieces of yourself to people who did not ask to be fed.
cards.keywords: cut watermelon, black seeds, red flesh, Viva la Vida, blue sky

cards.name: The Garden of Casa Azul
cards.meaning: Come home. The blue walls hold your history and the courtyard holds your breath. Every plant you ever tended is still growing. The garden does not care that you left — it kept living, and it will welcome you back without questions.
cards.reversedMeaning: The blue walls have become a prison painted to look like paradise. You call it sanctuary but you have not left in years. The garden grows inward, and so do you. Home becomes a cage when you are afraid of every other address.
cards.keywords: blue walls, courtyard, tropical plants, cobblestones, fountain

cards.name: The Xoloitzcuintli
cards.meaning: The ancient dog walks beside you toward the underworld and back again. It does not flinch at the dark. It does not judge the journey. Some guides have no words and no fur and no beauty — only loyalty older than language.
cards.reversedMeaning: You are following a guide that walks only toward the dead. The Xolo knows the path to the underworld too well and you have mistaken its comfort in darkness for wisdom. Not every descent is necessary. Some guides need to be called back.
cards.keywords: hairless dog, obsidian eyes, Mictlan, ancient breed, terracotta

cards.name: Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace
cards.meaning: You chose the thorns. Not because you enjoy the bleeding but because beauty without pain is a lie you are no longer willing to tell. The necklace draws blood at the throat — right where the voice lives. Speak anyway.
cards.reversedMeaning: Martyrdom as aesthetic. You have decorated yourself with suffering until the thorns look intentional, curated, beautiful. But the blood is still real and the wound at your throat is closing around words you actually need to say.
cards.keywords: thorn necklace, hummingbird, black cat, butterfly, blood drops

cards.name: Self-Portrait with Cropped Hair
cards.meaning: Cut it all off. The hair, the expectation, the version of yourself they loved. Sit in the chair in a man's suit with scissors in your hand and dare them to say you are less. You are not less. You are finally the right shape.
cards.reversedMeaning: Destruction disguised as liberation. You cut away everything that others loved but you did it in rage, not freedom. The hair on the floor is not a declaration — it is evidence. Reinvention driven by spite leaves you cold in someone else's suit.
cards.keywords: scissors, shorn hair, oversized suit, wooden chair, sheet music

cards.name: The Tehuana Dress
cards.meaning: Put on the armor that looks like a garden. The Tehuana dress is not costume — it is a country you wear on your body, a matriarchy stitched into every hem. You are allowed to construct yourself from the most powerful things you come from.
cards.reversedMeaning: The costume has replaced the self. You wear your heritage like a shield but you have forgotten the woman underneath the embroidery. Identity built entirely from symbols becomes its own kind of mask — beautiful, impenetrable, hollow.
cards.keywords: embroidered dress, headdress, lace, flowers, Zapotec textile

cards.name: The Unibrow
cards.meaning: Refuse to edit yourself for their comfort. The line across your brow is a bridge between two eyes that see everything — let it grow, let it darken, let it become the first thing they notice. Your refusal to disappear is its own kind of art.
cards.reversedMeaning: Defiance for its own sake. You have made a religion of refusal until you cannot tell the difference between authentic selfhood and performing rebellion. The unibrow is real, but are you wearing it or is it wearing you?
cards.keywords: dark brow, unflinching gaze, mirror, direct stare, wings of a bird

cards.name: The Diary
cards.meaning: Write it in ink that bleeds. The diary does not ask you to be coherent — it asks you to be true. Draw the pain in watercolor, write the love letter that contradicts the last page, let the book hold what your body cannot.
cards.reversedMeaning: The diary has become a courtroom. You write to build a case, to prove your suffering, to construct a version of events where you are always the wounded one. The pages are evidence now, not catharsis. Close the book and live unrecorded.
cards.keywords: open journal, watercolor sketches, ink stains, pressed flowers, handwriting

cards.name: The Palette
cards.meaning: The colors are your vocabulary. Mix them with oil and turpentine and fury and tenderness. What cannot be spoken can be painted, and what cannot be painted can be smeared across the canvas like a wound that refuses to dry.
cards.reversedMeaning: The palette has become an escape from living. You paint the wound instead of tending it, you mix colors instead of having conversations, you create beauty from pain without ever actually reducing the pain. Art is not medicine — it is testimony.
cards.keywords: oil palette, brushes, paint-stained hands, turpentine, cadmium red

cards.name: The Communist Heart
cards.meaning: Your heart beats for more than yourself. The conviction is not abstract — it lives in your chest alongside the love and the grief. Let your politics be as personal as your bloodstream. The revolution begins in the body.
cards.reversedMeaning: Ideology has replaced intimacy. You give your heart to the cause and leave nothing for the people in the room. The hammer and sickle hang where a pulse should be, and you have mistaken fervor for feeling.
cards.keywords: hammer and sickle, red star, clenched fist, heart, red flag

cards.name: The Ancestor
cards.meaning: You are not the first one to survive this. Behind you stands a line of faces — Aztec, mestiza, broken and rebuilt in every generation. Their blood is your inheritance and their endurance is already in your marrow. You were never starting from nothing.
cards.reversedMeaning: You are hiding behind the ancestors instead of standing beside them. Their strength has become your excuse — you invoke lineage to avoid building something of your own. The dead are generous, but they cannot live your life for you.
cards.keywords: pre-Columbian figure, family portrait, roots, obsidian mirror, braided hair

cards.name: The Bus
cards.meaning: The accident is the dividing line. Before the bus, you were one person. After, you were another. You did not choose this — the iron handrail chose you. But the person who emerged from the wreckage learned to paint. The worst thing that happened to you made you who you are.
cards.reversedMeaning: You are still on the bus. The crash happened years ago but you relive the impact daily, clutching the handrail, bracing for contact. The accident made you, yes — but if you do not leave the wreckage, it will be the only thing you ever are.
cards.keywords: trolley bus, iron handrail, gold dust, broken body, collision

cards.name: The Skeleton
cards.meaning: Death has been sitting on your bed this whole time, grinning and patient, wired with paper flowers. It is not a threat — it is the most honest companion you have. Let the skeleton laugh. You are still here, and that is the funniest joke of all.
cards.reversedMeaning: You have invited death too close to the pillow. The skeleton on the bed was a companion but now it is a comfort, and you reach for the bony hand more often than the living ones. Befriending death is wisdom. Preferring it is surrender.
cards.keywords: Judas skeleton, paper flowers, canopy bed, fireworks, grinning skull

cards.name: The Miscarriage
cards.meaning: Something grew inside you and could not stay. This is not failure — it is the body's terrible honesty. The blood is a language and it is saying: this was real, this mattered, and you are allowed to collapse under the weight of what left you.
cards.reversedMeaning: You carry the ghost of every almost. The unborn thing has become a haunting, and you punish your body for its honesty. Grief that turns to blame — of yourself, of the flesh, of the universe — is grief that has nowhere left to go.
cards.keywords: blood, hospital bed, fetus, red ribbon, crescent moon, tears

cards.name: What the Water Gave Me
cards.meaning: Look into the bathwater. Everything surfaces — the memories, the bodies, the buildings, the tiny catastrophes floating alongside the enormous ones. The water holds it all without judgment. Let yourself see what rises when you are finally still.
cards.reversedMeaning: You are drowning in reflection. The water that was meant to cleanse has become an obsession, and you stare at the surface until the memories multiply. Introspection without action is just a beautiful way to sink.
cards.keywords: bathtub, floating images, feet, volcano, skeleton, plants

cards.name: The Sun and Moon
cards.meaning: You contain both the burning and the cooling. Day-self and night-self, the one who creates and the one who dissolves. Neither is more true than the other. The eclipse is not a crisis — it is the moment you are finally whole.
cards.reversedMeaning: The sun and moon are at war inside you and you are the battlefield. You cannot reconcile the parts of yourself that contradict, and so you split — day-person, night-person — never whole, always performing half of what you are.
cards.keywords: eclipse, sun face, moon face, split sky, day and night

cards.name: The Offering
cards.meaning: Lay it on the altar — the marigolds, the sugar skull, the bread, the photograph of who you were. The offering is not for the dead. It is for you, the living one who needs a ritual large enough to hold what words cannot. Build the altar. Light the candle. Let the smoke carry it.
cards.reversedMeaning: The altar has become an obligation. You offer and offer but the ritual is empty now, performed from guilt rather than love. The marigolds are wilting because you forgot that offerings require presence, not just objects.
cards.keywords: marigolds, sugar skull, candles, copal smoke, ofrenda

cards.name: The Wings
cards.meaning: You have earned these. Not the wings of angels — the wings of a woman who rebuilt her own skeleton from paint and fury and refusal. The flying is not escape. It is the body finally doing what the spirit always knew it could. Rise.
cards.reversedMeaning: Icarus in a Tehuana dress. You have flown so far from the ground that you have forgotten what roots feel like. The wings are magnificent but they are made of wax and pigment and pride, and the sun has always been indifferent to ambition. Come down before you fall.
cards.keywords: feathered wings, rising figure, blue sky, sun, broken chains
